site.intro: A weekly NRL command board for sharper tipping, cleaner ladder strategy, and banking any wins for charity.

featurePanels.title: Ladder Target
items: Last supplied comp screenshot showed Prince_of_Penrith 3rd on 194 points and 5 points behind 2nd.
A fresh comp ladder screenshot is still needed to recalibrate the exact gap after the validated 8 from 8 Round 20 card.
The operating target remains 2nd or better before finals.
The board keeps protecting obvious favourite points while looking for one or two real team-news splits.

featurePanels.title: Round 20 Wrap
items: Validated Round 20 card: 8 correct from 8 official result lines.
Correct: Broncos over Panthers, Sharks over Knights, Roosters over Storm, Raiders over Rabbitohs, Warriors over Dragons, Bulldogs over Wests Tigers, Titans over Sea Eagles and Dolphins over Cowboys.
The best split calls were Broncos, Titans and Dolphins, which all landed.
No Round 20 charity bet was locked, so the Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains unchanged.

featurePanels.title: Market Read
items: Sportsbet head-to-head favourites for Round 21 are Panthers, Roosters, Rabbitohs, Raiders, Warriors, Broncos, Dragons and Sharks.
Leaders are likely to copy most of that favourite-heavy path after a volatile Round 20.
TipBot matches Panthers, Roosters, Raiders, Warriors, Broncos and Sharks.
The two controlled splits are Storm over Rabbitohs and Titans over Dragons.
Storm is the main edge: Sportsbet has Souths favourite, but the official NRL team list names Jahrome Hughes back while Souths lose Cameron Murray and Tallis Duncan.

featurePanels.title: Round 21 Tips
items: Panthers over Eels - protect the heavy market favourite.
Roosters over Knights - match the market with Tedesco back and Gagai out for Newcastle.
Storm over Rabbitohs - controlled split against the market because Jahrome Hughes is named and Souths have major middle/edge injuries.
Raiders over Wests Tigers - protect the heavy home favourite despite Hudson Young being out.
Warriors over Bulldogs - protect the top-two ladder side, but respect Canterbury's improved form.
Broncos over Cowboys - match the market; Brisbane's Round 20 upset gives this more than reputation value.
Titans over Dragons - tiny-market split; Gold Coast have more attack and confidence after beating Manly.
Sharks over Sea Eagles - protect with Cronulla healthier and Manly missing Tom Trbojevic.

betCard.title: Round 21 Charity Bet
betCard.kickoff: Round runs Thursday 23 July to Sunday 26 July 2026
why: This update is a tipping-comp strategy card, not a confirmed charity-bet slip.
Sportsbet head-to-head prices were used to model likely leader behaviour, not to place a bet.
A charity bet only becomes official after explicit user confirmation before kickoff.
risks: Do not convert the Storm differential into a charity bet without a fresh price and explicit confirmation.
Several Round 21 picks are late-mail sensitive, especially Rabbitohs v Storm, Raiders v Tigers and Dragons v Titans.
Any future charity play still needs recorded odds, stake, match, and explicit confirmation before kickoff.

sections.title: Situation Report
sections.heading: Round 20 paid off, now stay market-aware
lines: Round 20 has been validated as an 8 from 8 TipBot card from official NRL result lines.
The new Round 21 card runs from Thursday 23 July to Sunday 26 July 2026.
The Dolphins have the bye.
The last known comp target remains Prince_of_Penrith chasing a 5-point gap to 2nd, pending a fresh ladder screenshot after Round 20.
The weekly strategy remains: match clear favourites, then take only controlled, team-news-backed splits.

sections.title: Bet Analysis
sections.heading: No charity bet locked for Round 21
lines: The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger remains 3-2 with $31.50 invested, $32.03 returned and +$0.53 settled profit.
No Round 21 charity result should be added without a pre-recorded stake, price and explicit charity-bet confirmation.
Sportsbet head-to-head prices are used here for tipping-comp psychology and leader modelling.
The Storm pick is a ladder differential, not a charity-bet instruction.

sections.title: Round 21 Tips
sections.heading: Recommended card for Prince_of_Penrith
lines: Eels v Panthers - Panthers. Sportsbet has Penrith $1.19; Eels regain Mitchell Moses but Penrith still have Cleary, Yeo, Martin and their main spine intact.
Knights v Roosters - Roosters. Sportsbet has Roosters $1.72; Tedesco returns and Newcastle lose Dane Gagai to calf tightness.
Rabbitohs v Storm - Storm. Sportsbet has Souths $1.57 and Storm $2.41, but official teams name Jahrome Hughes back while Souths lose Cameron Murray and Tallis Duncan.
Raiders v Wests Tigers - Raiders. Sportsbet has Canberra $1.22; the Tigers are reshuffled and judiciary-dependent.
Bulldogs v Warriors - Warriors. Sportsbet has Warriors $1.51; Canterbury improve with Crichton and Kikau back, but the Warriors remain the stronger ladder side.
Cowboys v Broncos - Broncos. Sportsbet has Brisbane $1.77; Walsh, Mam, Reynolds, Haas and Carrigan backed up the Panthers upset and the Cowboys have lost five straight.
Dragons v Titans - Titans. Sportsbet has Dragons $1.90 and Titans $1.94; TipBot takes the tiny outsider after the Titans beat Manly and Dragons remain bottom.
Sea Eagles v Sharks - Sharks. Sportsbet has Cronulla $1.64; Manly are without Tom Trbojevic and Cronulla regain Jesse Ramien, Siosifa Talakai and Jesse Colquhoun.

Sea Eagles v Sharks - Sun 26 Jul, 4:05pm at 4 Pines Park.
Bye: Dolphins.

sections.title: Market Map
sections.heading: Likely leader-safe picks
lines: Sportsbet prices captured from the Round 21 NRL market page: Panthers $1.19, Roosters $1.72, Rabbitohs $1.57, Raiders $1.22, Warriors $1.51, Broncos $1.77, Dragons $1.90 and Sharks $1.64.
TipBot matches six of the eight favourite sides: Panthers, Roosters, Raiders, Warriors, Broncos and Sharks.
TipBot splits from the market on Storm over Rabbitohs because the official team list gives Melbourne Jahrome Hughes back and takes Cameron Murray out of Souths.
TipBot splits from the market on Titans over Dragons because the price is almost even and the Titans have better attacking upside.
Do not add more underdogs. Two splits is enough after an 8 from 8 week.

sections.title: Late Mail
sections.heading: Selection notes driving the card
lines: Eels: Mitchell Moses returns from a corked thigh; Ronald Volkman and Luca Moretti clear concussion protocols.
Panthers: Ivan Cleary keeps the same 19-man squad; Mitch Kenny still a couple of weeks away.
Roosters: James Tedesco and Rob Toia return; Cody Ramsey and Hugo Savala move to the bench.
Rabbitohs: Cameron Murray and Tallis Duncan are out; David Fifita is named pending judiciary.
Storm: Jahrome Hughes is back from hamstring soreness; Will Warbrick is out for the season.
Raiders: Hudson Young is out for the season and Owen Pattie is suspended, but Canberra still have the stronger home setup.
Tigers: Adam Doueihi and Latu Fainu sit in the reserves pending judiciary outcomes; Javon Andrews is named at halfback.
Sea Eagles: Lehi Hopoate replaces Tom Trbojevic at fullback; Sharks regain Jesse Ramien, Siosifa Talakai and Jesse Colquhoun.
